single_source_trades.reason_other_missed: Gemini did not analyze monthly CPI component; focused on YoY aggregate. Claude's monthly reading (0.3-0.4pp higher in April vs March's 0.9%) supported by energy shock catalyst and forward economist consensus.

Gemini's GDP trade targets T1.5 threshold (NO direction, 11% edge); Claude targets T2.0 (NO direction, 12% edge). These are different thresholds on same underlying variable. Gemini's 1.5% threshold is lower bar; Claude's 2.0% is higher bar. Claude's grounding (Atlanta Fed GDPNow at 2.0% as of late March, weak Q4 2025 base) is more specific and recent. Both agree direction (NO) but Gemini's edge is marginally lower and threshold less aggressive. Promote Claude's higher-edge version.

contradictions.explanation: Both agree GDP will NOT exceed threshold, but Claude targets 2.0% (prob_range 0.35-0.45 = 0.4 midpoint) vs Gemini targets 1.5% (prob_range 0.5-0.58 = 0.54 midpoint). Claude's estimate is more pessimistic and has higher edge (12% vs 11%). Ranges do not overlap. Claude's Atlanta Fed GDPNow source more recent and granular than Gemini's reference. Claude's position is stronger.

contradictions.market: Year-over-year CPI inflation threshold in April 2026
contradictions.explanation: Claude: KXCPIYOY-26APR-T3.5 YES with prob 0.8-0.9 (edge 12%). Gemini: KXCPIYOY-26APR-T3.4 NO with prob 0.6-0.7 (edge 16%). Different thresholds (3.5 vs 3.4) create semantic confusion. On substance: Claude cites March CPI at 3.3% YoY plus energy shock = pushes above 3.5%. Gemini cites disinflationary trends from Fed tightening, implying below 3.4%. Ranges do not overlap (0.8-0.9 vs 0.6-0.7). Gemini's disinflationary thesis contradicts explicit March 3.3% baseline and acknowledged geopolitical shock risk. Claude's reasoning more grounded in recent data. Direction mismatch effectively present despite different thresholds.

contradictions.market: CPI inflation above 3.4-3.5% for year ending April 2026
contradictions.explanation: Python validator caught non-overlapping ranges misclassified as consensus

portfolio_concentration_warning: Inflation cluster at weight 9 approaches limit of 5. Two inflation trades (YoY CPI and monthly CPI) are highly correlated via common energy shock catalyst. Monthly CPI (KXCPI-26APR-T0.4) is single-source with weight 3; YoY CPI (KXCPIYOY-26APR-T3.5) is consensus with weight 6. Recommend reducing YoY CPI to weight 5 or reducing monthly CPI to weight 1 to respect cluster cap. Alternatively, consider one trade sufficient given high correlation. Energy shock is primary driver of both; resolution dates overlap (May 1-12). Execution risk if oil shock moderates faster than expected. GDP trade (weight 2) provides modest diversification.
reeval_triggers: CPI print on 2026-05-12: If April CPI comes in ≤0.2% MoM or YoY reading ≤3.3%, reverse both inflation trades immediately.
Oil price shock resolution: If Iran ceasefire materializes or Brent crude falls below $80/bbl, reassess energy-driven inflation catalyst; likely margin compression in April CPI prints.
Atlanta Fed GDPNow update after April 18: If GDPNow revises Q1 2026 above 2.2%, exit KXGDP-26APR30-T2.0 NO.
Fed communications: Any hawkish surprise (rate hold signaling continued tightening) strengthens Gemini's disinflationary thesis; consider hedging YoY CPI YES with small counter-position.
Implied probabilities converge below consensus range: If KXCPIYOY-26APR-T3.5 price declines below 0.75, re-evaluate market repricing of inflation risk.
